anthropic.note: Enforced via daily lockfile /tmp/audit_check_ai_last_run.txt — at most one audit fire per UTC day. Each fire = 1 Opus extended-thinking call (~$2). No Haiku callers in active code; if reintroduced, pair with explicit enforcement and update this cap.

gemini.note: Hard-coded in gemma4_helper.py _GEMINI_DAILY_LIMIT (added 2026-04-25). Currently only audit_check + audit_fire_debate use it; cap has plenty of headroom.

ai_consensus.gemini:
**(a) Runaway Loops:** Anthropic uses a `/tmp/` lockfile. If deployed in ephemeral containers, `/tmp` wipes on every restart. A crashing bot will endlessly restart, bypass the wiped lock, and spam $2 Opus calls. *If my ephemeral forecast is wrong:* OS-level permission errors on `/tmp` could still cause silent fail-opens.

**(b) Missing Caps:** Zero projected calls despite 24 timers means the static scanner completely missed dynamic trading triggers. No platform-level API gateway hard-caps exist, only brittle local code.

**(c) 2x Rate Blow-up:** Gemini doubling (if its in-memory limit resets on crash) is cheap. Anthropic doubling proves the lockfile failed, signaling an imminent infinite loop blow-up.

**(d) Invisible Burn:** Anthropic Opus. Background "audit fires" ($2/call) will silently
ai_consensus.anthropic:
**Blow-up scenarios:**

(a) **Runaway loops:** Lockfile `/tmp/audit_check_ai_last_run.txt` is on tmpfs — a reboot/container restart wipes it, allowing multiple Opus fires/day. Also vulnerable to clock skew at UTC rollover and concurrent invocations (no flock).

(b) **Missing caps:** 24 timers discovered but `projected.sources=[]` — auditor can't see them. Anthropic cap=1 only protects audit_check; any *new* Opus caller bypasses it (no global SDK wrapper). Gemini=100/day is per-helper, not per-key.

(c) **2× rate blow-up:** Anthropic → $4/day ($1.4k/yr) silently; Gemini doubling stays under cap, masked.

(d) **Most likely silent burn:** `audit_fire_debate` — multi-turn Gemini debates can fan out per fire; no per-call logging visible here, and it shares the cap pool with audit_check.

ai_consensus.grok:
**Blow-up risks:**

(a) **Runaway loops**: 24 discovered timers + fragile /tmp lockfile creates race conditions. Concurrent runs or lockfile deletion (common in containers/reboots) bypass the "one Opus call per UTC day" rule.

(b) **Missing daily caps**: Gemini's hard-coded 100/day limit only covers two callers. New code paths or Haiku reintroduction have zero enforcement.

(c) **2x rate blow-up**: Anthropic jumps from $2 → $4/day (lockfile easily defeated). Gemini at 200 calls/day has unknown per-call cost but guaranteed quota breach.

(d) **Silent burner**: **Anthropic** — $2 Opus calls via audit_fire can run multiple times daily with no operator visibility if lockfile is absent. Highest stealth risk. 

**Overall**: "LOW" rating is misleading. Lockfile is single point of failure.
